items.hook: Danny Boyle’s ‘Ink’ to open Venice Film Festival 2026
items.body: Jack O’Connell stars in the film about the early days of Rupert Murdoch’s media empire.

items.hook: Toronto adds special presentations including ‘Alpha Gang’, ‘Love Of Your Life’
items.body: Other world premieres joining the roster include Ben Shirinian’s ‘The Housewife’.

items.hook: Edward Leon Rowny was a United States Army lieutenant general of Polish extraction.
items.body: He was a commanding officer in World War II and Korea, a military advisor to five United States presidents and a negotiator on the Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Worry Doll by Laura McPhee-Browne review – a sensual, sinister novel about the horrors of desire
items.body:
Two women meet on a train and tumble into an all-consuming affair told from both sides – who have very different stories

items.hook: A voyage of discovery: an idiot’s guide to reading The Odyssey
items.body:
Christopher Nolan’s film adaptation of the ancient Greek epic has sparked a new appetite for an old classic. Here are the translations, podcasts and audiobooks that make the Homeric world more approachable

The Odyssey was once all

items.hook: Chaplain–Medic massacre: American POWs are massacred by North Korean Army.
items.body: The Chaplain–Medic massacre took place in the Korean War on July 16, 1950, on a mountain above the village of Tuman. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: King Richard II of England is crowned.
items.body: Richard II, also known as Richard of Bordeaux, was King of England from 1377 until he was deposed in 1399. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Cathedral of Saint Joseph is a Catholic cathedral serving as the mother church for the Diocese of Jefferson City in Missouri in the United States.
items.body: It serves as the seat of the bishop, Ralph Bernard O'Donnell. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Interview: Nader Saeivar • Director of Hijamat - “I wasn’t interested in judging, I wanted to observe how mechanisms continue to shape people’s lives” - Karlovy Vary 2026 - Competition
items.body: The filmmaker discusses his latest film, centred on a traditional Muslim family and community whose world is shaken when they discover that one of their young members is gay

items.hook: The comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 is destroyed in a head-on collision with Jupiter.
items.body: Comet Shoemaker–Levy 9 was a comet that broke apart in July 1992 and collided with Jupiter in July 1994, providing the first direct observation of an extraterrestrial collision of Solar System objects. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: National Year of Reading should extend to a decade, inquiry says
items.body:
The education committee also proposed a National Reading Guarantee, ensuring all children have frequent opportunities to enjoy reading

The National Year of Reading should be extended to a National Decade of Reading, an education co

items.hook: Manhattan Project: The Atomic Age begins when the United States successfully detonates a plutonium-based test nuclear weapon near Alamogordo, New Mexico.
items.body: The Manhattan Project was a research and development program undertaken during World War II to produce the first nuclear weapons. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The School Census is a statutory data collection for all maintained (state-funded) schools in England.
items.body: This includes nursery, primary, secondary, middle-deemed primary, middle-deemed secondary, local authority maintained special and non-maintained special schools, academies including free schools, studio schools and university technical colleges and city techno

items.hook: The Spanish town of Arjona declares independence and names its native Muhammad ibn Yusuf as ruler. This marks the Muhammad's first rise to prominence; he later established the Nasrid Emirate of Granada, the last independent Muslim state in Spain.
items.body: Arjona is a municipality in the province of Jaén, Andalusia, Spain. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: J. D. Salinger publishes his popular yet controversial novel, The Catcher in the Rye.
items.body: Jerome David Salinger was an American author best known for his 1951 novel The Catcher in the Rye. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Sampson was a Shire horse gelding born in 1846 and bred by Thomas Cleaver at Toddington Mills, Bedfordshire, England.
items.body: According to Guinness World Records (1986), he was the tallest horse ever recorded, by 1850 measuring 219.7 centimetres or 21.2½ hands in height. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: NBC Nightly News is the flagship daily evening television news program for NBC News, the news division of the NBC television network in the United States.
items.body: First aired on August 3, 1970, the program is currently the second most-watched network newscast in the United States, behind ABC's World News Tonight. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
